title: ES Mock 02 — Air & Noise Pollution Heavy

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct preposition: 'The ore sample was analysed ____ traces of gold.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Analyse for' is the idiomatic collocation when testing for the presence of something.

**Working.** We test a sample 'for' a substance; the other prepositions do not fit this sense.

**Answer.** for.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.topic: Quantitative Aptitude — Time, Speed and Distance
questions.stem: A car travels $180$ km in $3$ hours. Its average speed is _____ km/h.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Speed = distance / time.

**Working.** $\dfrac{180}{3}=60$ km/h.

**Answer.** $60$ km/h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An amount of $600$ is divided in the ratio $2:3$. The larger part is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each share equals the total times its ratio fraction.

**Working.** Larger fraction $=\dfrac{3}{2+3}=\dfrac{3}{5}$, so
$$600\times\frac{3}{5}=360$$

**Answer.** $360$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: What is $35\%$ of $240$?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Percent means parts per hundred.

**Working.**
$$\frac{35}{100}\times 240 = 84$$

**Answer.** $84$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A sector representing $25\%$ of a total is shown shaded on the pie chart. Compute its central angle (degrees).

figure.caption: Pie sector
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M85 80 L85 20 A60 60 0 0 1 145 80 Z' fill='#2563eb'/><text x='30' y='158' font-size='8'>shaded = 25% of pie</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Central angle $=\text{fraction}\times360^\circ$.

**Working.**
$$\theta=0.25\times360=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Convert to the passive voice: 'The committee approved the safety proposal.'
options: The safety proposal was approved by the committee.
The safety proposal is approved by the committee.
The safety proposal has approved the committee.
The committee was approved by the safety proposal.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Passive voice: object becomes subject, verb becomes 'be + past participle', and the tense is preserved.

**Working.** Simple past 'approved' becomes 'was approved'; the object 'proposal' moves to the front.

**Answer.** 'The safety proposal was approved by the committee.'

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The area of a circle of radius $7$ is _____ (use $\pi=22/7$).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Area $=\pi r^2$.

**Working.** $\dfrac{22}{7}\times 7^2 = 22\times 7 = 154$.

**Answer.** $154$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next letter in the series: A, C, E, G, ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The letters advance by two positions each time (odd-positioned letters).

**Working.** After G (7th letter), skip H to reach I.

**Answer.** I.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The net shown is folded into a cube. Which face is opposite face 2?

figure.caption: A cube net: a horizontal row of faces 1, 2, 3, 4 with face 5 above face 2 and face 6 below face 2.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2' fill='rgba(99,102,241,0.08)'><rect x='20' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='100' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='140' y='70' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='30' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='110' width='40' height='40'/></g><g font-size='16' fill='#334155'><text x='36' y='95'>1</text><text x='76' y='95'>2</text><text x='116' y='95'>3</text><text x='156' y='95'>4</text><text x='76' y='55'>5</text><text x='76' y='135'>6</text></g></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a straight row of four faces, faces two apart are opposite.

**Working.** With 2 as the front, 1 is left, 3 is right and 4 is back; front (2) is opposite back (4).

**Answer.** 4.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A train $200$ m long moving at $36$ km/h crosses a platform $100$ m long in _____ seconds.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** To cross a platform, the train covers (its length + platform length).

**Working.** Speed $=36\times\tfrac{5}{18}=10$ m/s; distance $=200+100=300$ m; time $=\dfrac{300}{10}=30$ s.

**Answer.** $30$ s.

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $3$ identical sources each $85\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$85+10\log3=89.77.$$

**Answer.** $89.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A source emits $12\,\text{t}$ of a greenhouse gas. Reading the gas's GWP₁₀₀ from the figure, its CO₂-equivalent emission is _____ t CO₂-e.

**Concept.** CO₂-e = mass × GWP, with the GWP read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$12\times28=336.$$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: Which stage of the EIA cycle immediately follows screening?

figure.caption: Sequential stages of the EIA process.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Order: screening → scoping → impact prediction → EMP → review/clearance.

**Working.**
Scoping follows screening.

**Answer.** Scoping

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $2000\,\text{kg}$ at $40\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$2000(1-40/100)=1200.$$

**Answer.** $1200\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays by first-order kinetics with rate constant $k=0.1\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Its half-life is _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For first order, $t_{1/2}=\dfrac{\ln 2}{k}$.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.1}=6.93.$$

**Answer.** $6.93\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 500 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 2 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $v_s=\text{depth}\div\text{time}$, both read from the trajectory.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{2\,\text{m}}{500\,\text{s}}\times1000=4\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $4\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: Which are recognised biodiversity hotspots in/around India?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Western Ghats, Himalaya and Indo-Burma are global hotspots; the Thar is not (low endemism/richness threshold).

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** Western Ghats, Himalaya, Indo-Burma

questions.stem: MPN (Most Probable Number) test in water analysis estimates the density of
questions.solution:

**Working.**

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">9</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>

questions.stem: The nutrient most commonly limiting and therefore triggering eutrophication in freshwater lakes is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Phosphorus is usually the limiting nutrient in freshwater; its loading drives algal blooms.

**Working.**
Phosphorus.

**Answer.** Phosphorus

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=120\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 50 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 6 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 25 m,  σ_z = 25 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120}{\pi\times6\times25\times25}e^{-50^2/(2\times25^2)}=0.00137851.$$

**Answer.** $13.785\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: $3$ identical sources each $70\,\text{dB}$. Combined level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$70+10\log3=74.77.$$

**Answer.** $74.77\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.3\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $5$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="75" width="60" height="75" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 150</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$150e^{-0.3\times5}=33.47.$$

**Answer.** $33.47$

questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows logistically at $r=0.4\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the carrying capacity $K$ from the growth curve shown, the maximum $dN/dt$ ($rK/4$) is _____ /yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="34" y1="20" x2="34" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="34" y1="40" x2="305" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="240" y="36" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">K = 6000</text><path d="M34 158 C120 156 150 80 175 60 C210 44 260 41 305 40" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><text x="10" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="285" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><text x="120" y="120" font-size="8" fill="#16a34a">max dN/dt at N=K/2</text></svg>
figure.caption: Carrying capacity K marked on the growth curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** max dN/dt = rK/4 at N=K/2, with K read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.4\times6000}{4}=600.$$

**Answer.** $600$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.05\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $15$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 500</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$500e^{0.05\times15}=1059.$$

**Answer.** $1059$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Waste of mass $1000\,\text{kg}$ at loose density $100\,\text{kg/m}^3$ is compacted to $500\,\text{kg/m}^3$. The volume reduction is _____ %.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $VR=\left(1-\dfrac{V_2}{V_1}\right)\times100=\left(1-\dfrac{\rho_1}{\rho_2}\right)\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{100}{500}\right)\times100=80\%.$$

**Answer.** $80\%$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=40\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.3</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.3\times40}{50+40}=0.133.$$

**Answer.** $0.133$

**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

questions.solution:

**Answer.** Inversion, low mixing height, calm winds

questions.stem: A refrigerant recovery releases $2\,\text{t}$ of CFC-11 (ODP $=1.0$) and $5\,\text{t}$ of HCFC-22 (ODP $=0.055$). The total release expressed as CFC-11-equivalent (ODP-weighted) is _____ t.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ODP-weighted release $=\sum(\text{mass}\times\text{ODP})$ — the basis on which the Montreal Protocol controls ozone-depleting substances.

**Working.**
$$2(1.0)+5(0.055)=2.275\,\text{t}.$$

**Answer.** $2.275\,\text{t CFC-11-eq}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.08\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $13$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 1000</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$1000e^{0.08\times13}=2829.$$

**Answer.** $2829$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: When the environmental lapse rate is greater than the dry adiabatic lapse rate, the atmosphere is

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="10" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">altitude</text><text x="245" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">temperature →</text><line x1="120" y1="160" x2="220" y2="30" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3" stroke-dasharray="5 3"/><text x="222" y="34" font-size="8" fill="#475569">DALR (dry adiabatic)</text><line x1="150" y1="160" x2="200" y2="30" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.6"/><text x="172" y="50" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">ELR (unstable)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Environmental lapse rate vs. dry adiabatic lapse rate — unstable atmosphere.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** ELR > DALR ⇒ a displaced parcel keeps rising ⇒ superadiabatic, unstable (good dispersion, looping plume).

**Working.**
Superadiabatic, unstable.

**Answer.** Superadiabatic / unstable

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=80\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 30 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 5 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 30 m,  σ_z = 20 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{80}{\pi\times5\times30\times20}e^{-30^2/(2\times20^2)}=0.00275574.$$

**Answer.** $27.557\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹50,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 5</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{50000}{(1+0.08)^{5}}=34029.$$

**Answer.** $₹34029$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: In the energy pyramid shown, only the producer (trophic-1) energy is marked; 10% transfers per level. Energy at trophic level $4$ is _____ kcal/m²/yr.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 180" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><polygon points="40,150 280,150 240,118 80,118" fill="#16a34a" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="80,118 240,118 205,86 115,86" fill="#65a30d" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="115,86 205,86 178,54 142,54" fill="#d97706" stroke="#1e293b"/><polygon points="142,54 178,54 165,30 155,30" fill="#dc2626" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="160" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">Producers = 10000</text><text x="160" y="110" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L2 = ?</text><text x="160" y="78" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L3 = ?</text><text x="160" y="46" font-size="7" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">L4 = ?</text><text x="160" y="172" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kcal/m²/yr — 10% transfer per level</text></svg>
figure.caption: Only the producer (trophic level 1) energy is marked; upper levels follow the 10% rule. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Eₙ = E₀(0.1)ⁿ, with E₀ read from the pyramid.

**Working.**
$$10000\times0.1^{3}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: Alkalinity of natural water is primarily a measure of its capacity to

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="18" x2="38" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="160" x2="305" y2="160" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="14" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="240" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant added →</text><line x1="38" y1="89" x2="305" y2="89" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 3"/><text x="42" y="86" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">pH 7</text><path d="M38 150 C120 146 160 140 168 89 C176 38 230 32 305 28" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="168" cy="89" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence pt</text></svg>
figure.caption: Titration curve: sharp pH jump at the equivalence point.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Alkalinity = acid-neutralising capacity (mainly HCO₃⁻/CO₃²⁻/OH⁻).

**Working.**
Acid-neutralising capacity.

**Answer.** Neutralise acid

questions.solution:
**Concept.** F/M = QS₀/(VX).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{12000\times200}{2000\times3000}=0.4.$$

**Answer.** $0.4$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1000\,\text{kg}$ at $60\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1000(1-60/100)=400.$$

**Answer.** $400\,\text{kg}$
